entry.title: SAP Generative AI Hub: A Practitioner's Setup Guide
entry.content:
SAP COMMUNITY  |  PRACTICAL GUIDE  |  SAP BTP  |  SAP AI CORE

SAP Generative AI Hub: A Practitioner's Setup Guide

Step-by-step configuration from BTP entitlements to your first foundation model API call — including the friction points that aren't in the official documentation.

By Deepa Dorairaj   |  SAP AI Solution Architect  |  SAP BTP & AI Core  |  Published 2026

TL;DR: SAP Generative AI Hub gives you governed, auditable access to GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, Mistral, and SAP's own models from a single BTP service. Getting there requires navigating BTP entitlements, AI Core service instance setup, resource group configuration, and API authentication — none of which is as straightforward as the documentation suggests. This guide covers the complete setup path and the specific friction points that will slow you down if you don't know about them.

What the Generative AI Hub Actually Is

SAP's Generative AI Hub is the access layer within SAP AI Core that gives you a single, governed interface to multiple foundation models — GPT-4o via Azure OpenAI, Claude from Anthropic, Gemini from Google, Mistral, SAP-RPT-1, and others — without building separate integrations to each provider. It handles authentication, usage tracking, rate limiting, and compliance logging in a way that meets enterprise governance requirements.

For SAP architects and data engineers, the key value proposition is straightforward: instead of managing separate API keys, separate billing accounts, and separate compliance considerations for each AI provider, the Generative AI Hub abstracts all of that into a single BTP service with consistent access patterns, audit trails, and cost visibility.

What the documentation does not prepare you for is how much configuration stands between you and your first working API call. This guide covers every step — including the ones that aren't clearly documented — so you can get from zero to a working foundation model integration without the friction we went through.

BEFORE YOU START 

Prerequisites and What You Actually Need

Before beginning setup, confirm you have the following. Missing any of these is the most common reason setup stalls before it starts:

  • A BTP global account with available entitlements — a trial account works for initial experimentation but has model and quota limitations
  • A BTP subaccount in a supported region — AI Core availability varies by region and not all models are available in all regions (covered in detail below)
  • Space Manager or Organization Manager role in your BTP subaccount — standard Developer roles are insufficient for service instance creation
  • Access to the BTP Cockpit — your entry point for all entitlement and service configuration

Region matters more than you might expect. GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini availability through the Generative AI Hub is not uniform across all BTP regions. US East, EU West, and AP regions have the broadest model availability. If you set up AI Core in an unsupported region, you will successfully create the service instance but find that certain models are simply not visible in the Hub. Check SAP's current model availability matrix before choosing your subaccount region.

STEP 1 OF 5 

BTP Entitlement Setup

Adding the AI Core Entitlement

Navigate to your BTP global account and open the Entitlements section. This is where most teams hit their first friction point — the AI Core service and the Generative AI Hub are separate entitlements and both need to be explicitly added before you can create a service instance.

In the BTP Cockpit, go to Entitlements → Entity Assignments → select your subaccount → Configure Entitlements → Add Service Plans.

Search for and add the following:

  • SAP AI Core — select the Extended service plan (not the Free tier, which has significant model and quota restrictions)
  • SAP AI Launchpad — needed to access the Generative AI Hub UI; easy to miss if you assume the Hub is part of AI Core itself

The most common entitlement mistake: adding SAP AI Core but not SAP AI Launchpad. AI Core is the runtime service. AI Launchpad is the UI that gives you access to the Generative AI Hub prompt editor and model catalog. Without AI Launchpad, you can make API calls but you cannot use the visual interface for prompt experimentation. Add both entitlements before proceeding.

Quota Allocation

After adding the entitlements, allocate quota to your subaccount. The default quota allocation for AI Core is zero — you must explicitly set it. In the Entitlements screen, set the quota for the Extended plan to at least 1 unit to enable service instance creation.

Quota and billing transparency is a genuine friction point here. The Generative AI Hub bills on a token consumption basis per model, but the BTP entitlement setup does not show you projected costs or usage limits upfront. Before running any workloads in production, contact your SAP account team to understand the cost model for your specific contract — trial and CPEA accounts have different billing mechanics.

 

Step

Action

Detail

1

Open BTP Cockpit

Navigate to your global account → Subaccounts → select your subaccount

2

Open Entitlements

Entitlements → Entity Assignments → Configure Entitlements

3

Add AI Core

Add Service Plans → search 'SAP AI Core' → select Extended plan

4

Add AI Launchpad

Add Service Plans → search 'SAP AI Launchpad' → select Standard plan

5

Set quota

Set AI Core Extended plan quota to minimum 1 unit → Save

STEP 2 OF 5 

Creating the AI Core Service Instance

Service Instance Creation

With entitlements in place, navigate to your BTP subaccount → Services → Service Marketplace → search for SAP AI Core → select the Extended plan → Create.

During instance creation you will be prompted to provide a JSON configuration parameter. For standard Generative AI Hub access, the default configuration is sufficient — do not skip this step by leaving it empty, as certain model access features require the configuration to be present even if it is set to default values.

{

  "serviceName": "aicore",

  "description": "AI Core instance for Generative AI Hub access"

}

Creating a Service Key

After the service instance is created, you need a service key to generate the credentials your applications will use for API authentication. In the service instance detail view, select Service Keys → Create → provide a name → Create.

The service key JSON contains the clientid, clientsecret, url (for OAuth token endpoint), and serviceurls.AI_API_URL (your AI Core API base URL). Save this immediately and store it securely — you will need all four values for API authentication.

API authentication is more complex than a simple API key. AI Core uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow. Your application must first call the token endpoint with the clientid and clientsecret to obtain a bearer token, then include that token in each AI Core API call. Tokens expire and must be refreshed. Many teams expect a simple API key pattern and are surprised by the OAuth requirement. Plan your authentication implementation before writing any AI logic.

STEP 3 OF 5 

Resource Group Configuration

What Resource Groups Are and Why They Matter

A resource group in SAP AI Core is a logical isolation boundary for AI workloads — models, deployments, and executions run within a resource group context. It is not obvious from the documentation that resource group configuration is a prerequisite for accessing the Generative AI Hub, but it is. Without a configured resource group, model deployments will not be visible even after AI Core and AI Launchpad are correctly set up.

Resource group configuration is done through the AI Core API, not through the BTP Cockpit UI — which is another friction point teams don't anticipate. You will need to make a direct API call to create your resource group using the service key credentials you obtained in Step 2.

Creating the Resource Group via API

First, obtain an OAuth token using your service key credentials:

# Step 1: Get OAuth token

curl -X POST '<token_url>/oauth/token' \

  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \

  -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \

  -d 'client_id=<clientid>' \

  -d 'client_secret=<clientsecret>'

 

Then create your resource group using the bearer token:

# Step 2: Create resource group

curl -X PUT '<AI_API_URL>/v2/admin/resourceGroups' \

  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \

  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

  -d '{"resourceGroupId": "default"}'

Once the resource group is created, return to the AI Launchpad. You should now see the resource group available in the dropdown. Select it and navigate to the Generative AI Hub — the model catalog will now be visible and accessible.

STEP 4 OF 5 

Accessing the Generative AI Hub and Available Models

Navigating to the Hub

With AI Launchpad subscribed and your resource group configured, navigate to your BTP subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions → SAP AI Launchpad → Go to Application. Select your AI Core connection, choose your resource group, and open the Generative AI Hub from the left navigation.

The Hub has two primary sections you will use immediately: the Chat interface for interactive prompt testing and the Prompt Editor for structured experimentation with system prompts, parameters, and response comparison across models.

Available Foundation Models

The following models were available and tested during our setup. Model availability evolves — check the SAP AI Core release notes for the current catalog as new models are added regularly:

 

Model

Provider

Type

Best For

GPT-4o

Azure OpenAI

General LLM

Complex reasoning, code generation, document analysis

GPT-4o mini

Azure OpenAI

General LLM

Cost-efficient tasks, high-volume processing

Claude 3.5 Sonnet

Anthropic

General LLM

Long context, nuanced analysis, structured output

Claude 3 Haiku

Anthropic

General LLM

Fast, cost-efficient, high-volume classification

Gemini 1.5 Pro

Google

General LLM

Multimodal, very long context window

Mistral Large

Mistral AI

General LLM

European data residency, strong multilingual

Mistral Small

Mistral AI

General LLM

Cost-efficient European-hosted option

SAP-RPT-1

SAP

Tabular/structured

Numerical predictions on SAP tabular data

Not all models are available in all regions. If a model you expect to see is missing from your Hub catalog, the most likely cause is that your AI Core instance is in a region where that model is not yet deployed. This is not always clearly surfaced in the UI — the model simply does not appear rather than showing as unavailable. Check SAP's regional availability documentation if your expected models are missing.

STEP 5 OF 5 

Making Your First API Call

Using the Prompt Editor for Experimentation

Before writing any code, use the Generative AI Hub's built-in Prompt Editor to verify your setup is working and to experiment with models. The Prompt Editor allows you to set a system prompt, write a user message, adjust temperature and max token parameters, and compare responses across multiple models side by side.

This is the fastest way to validate that your entitlements, service instance, resource group, and model access are all correctly configured before investing time in application code.

Direct API Integration

For application integration, the AI Core API follows a consistent pattern across all models. After obtaining an OAuth token, construct your request to the chat completions endpoint:

import requests

# Step 1: Get OAuth bearer token

token_response = requests.post(

    f'{token_url}/oauth/token',

    data={

        'grant_type': 'client_credentials',

        'client_id': client_id,

        'client_secret': client_secret

    }

)

bearer_token = token_response.json()['access_token']

# Step 2: Call foundation model via AI Core API

headers = {

    'Authorization': f'Bearer {bearer_token}',

    'Content-Type': 'application/json',

    'AI-Resource-Group': 'default'

}

 

payload = {

    'model': 'gpt-4o',

    'messages': [

        {'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are an SAP data analyst.'},

        {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Summarize this SAP exception log: ...'}

    ],

    'max_tokens': 500,

    'temperature': 0.3

}

 

response = requests.post(

    f'{ai_api_url}/v2/inference/deployments/<deployment_id>/chat/completions',

    headers=headers,

    json=payload

)

print(response.json()['choices'][0]['message']['content'])

The deployment_id in the API endpoint URL is model-specific and changes when SAP updates model versions. Retrieve current deployment IDs programmatically from the AI Core API rather than hardcoding them — hardcoded deployment IDs are the most common cause of sudden API failures after a model version update.

Switching Between Models

The same API pattern works across all models in the Generative AI Hub — only the model parameter and deployment_id change. This is the core value of the Hub's abstraction layer: your application code does not need to change when you switch from GPT-4o to Claude or Mistral. The authentication, request structure, and response format are consistent.

The Friction Points: A Summary

For teams setting up the Generative AI Hub for the first time, these are the specific issues that will slow you down if you don't know about them upfront:

 

Step

Action

Detail

1

Missing AI Launchpad entitlement

AI Core alone is not sufficient — add SAP AI Launchpad separately or you will have no UI access to the Hub

2

Zero default quota

BTP allocates zero quota by default — explicitly set quota to at least 1 unit after adding the entitlement

3

Resource group not created

Models are invisible in the Hub until a resource group exists — create via API call before opening the Launchpad

4

OAuth not simple API key

AI Core uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials — implement token refresh logic before writing AI application code

5

Regional model gaps

Models missing from your catalog are likely unavailable in your region — check availability before choosing subaccount region

6

Hardcoded deployment IDs

Deployment IDs change with model updates — retrieve programmatically to avoid silent failures after SAP updates

What You Can Build From Here

With the Generative AI Hub correctly configured, the path to production SAP AI applications becomes significantly clearer. The governed, multi-model access layer means your team can experiment across GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, and Mistral without managing separate provider relationships, and switch models based on performance, cost, or compliance requirements without rewriting application code.

The natural next steps from this setup are connecting the Generative AI Hub to your HANA Cloud vector store for RAG-based applications on SAP data, building SAP Joule extensions that leverage foundation model capabilities, and integrating AI Core into your BTP CAP application workflows for intelligent enterprise applications.

The setup friction is real but it is a one-time cost. Once the entitlements, service instance, resource group, and authentication are in place, the development experience is significantly smoother than managing individual AI provider integrations.

If you hit any of the friction points listed above or encounter issues not covered here, drop a comment below. These are the setups where community knowledge compounds quickly — your specific error message or configuration issue is likely something another practitioner has already solved.

entry.title: 📅 Save the Date — SAP Inside Track Belgium 2026
entry.content:
🏠Returning to where it all started

Hello SAP Community! 👋

We are thrilled to announce that SAP Inside Track Belgium is back in 2026 — and this time, we're going back to our roots. 🎉

We're heading back to Kontich, to the offices of Flexso at Prins Boudewijnlaan 24A — a place that holds a very special place in the history of #sitBE. There were editions here before my time organized by others, but for me personally, this is where it all started. Now we're writing the next chapter — together.

📋Event Details

🗓️ Date: Saturday, September 19th, 2026

📍Location: Flexso — Prins Boudewijnlaan 24A, 2550 Kontich, Belgium 🇧🇪

🌐Website: https://sitbe.fiddle.be

🤔What is SAP Inside Track?

An SAP Inside Track is a free, community-driven event open to everyone — customers, consultants, freelancers, developers, architects, and business professionals alike. The event is organized by peers, for peers. Sessions are driven by knowledge sharing, not sales pitches. Come to learn, share, connect, and be inspired.

🏢Hosted by Flexso

A heartfelt thank you to Flexso for opening their doors and welcoming the SAP community back. Flexso is an expert SAP partner and a strong supporter of the SAP community in Belgium, located right next to the E19 in Kontich.

🌐https://www.flexso.com/en/flexso-kontich

🙋 Get Involved

🎤Speak at #sitBE — A Call for Speakers will open soon. 20-min lightning talks and 40-min deep dives are both welcome!

🎫Register to attend — Spots are limited. Registration details coming soon — stay tuned!

📢Spread the word — Share this post and tag your SAP colleagues. The more the merrier!

We can't wait to see you in Kontich on September 19th. Mark your calendars!

entry.title: Mastering Data Loads in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 - Proven Strategies for Accuracy & Performance
entry.content:
In this blog post, let’s discuss the best practices and recommendations to be followed while performing data loads into SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2. Data loading is one of the most critical activities in any implementation or migration project—whether you are onboarding master data, preparing for a cutover, migrating from V1, or integrating with SAP S/4HANA. A well-executed data load ensures a clean, reliable, and scalable foundation for all downstream business processes. On the other hand, poorly sequenced loads, missing External IDs, misconfigured rate limits, or active automations can cause message failures, performance degradation, and even business downtime, as seen in several real-world cases across customers. For this reason, following a structured, proven approach is essential to ensure consistency, avoid rework, and guarantee a smooth go‑live.


Recommendations for Data Loads via REST API calls

  • Rate limit is applicable for this type of data loads. Check the section Rate Limit to understand more
  • Deactivate the Autoflows for the entities you load the data
  • Deactivate the pre/post hooks if not necessary
  • Disable standard events for the data loading entity and related entities.
  • Deactivate the outbound integration to SAP S4HANA or ERP by disabling the corresponding communication system
  • Ensure you use an API user for these types of calls – Using API user will have lot of benefits in terms of performance and helps in faster data loads.

Note: Using Business User for this data loading is prohibited as it may cause performance problems and delays.


Recommendations for Data Loads via Data Impex tool (Import Create or Import Update)

  • Rate limit is NOT applicable for this type of data load
  • Deactivate the outbound integration by disabling the communication system and/or communication configuration, so the data is not sent to connected S4HANA or ERP systems
  • Deactivate the autoflows that may trigger events to external systems
  • Disable the standard events for the data loading entity and related entities
  • Deactivate the pre/post hooks if not necessary. Using pre/post hooks during the data loads will lead to higher processing times.


Recommendations for Data Loads using Pre-Packaged Integration (S4HANA or ERP)

  • Rate limit is applicable. Check below for more details
  • Deactivate the autoflows for the entities you load the data
  • Deactivate the pre/post hooks if not necessary. Using pre/post hooks during the data loads will lead to higher processing times.
  • Disable the standard events for the data loading entity and related entities

Master Data Loads from S4HANA or ERP

  • Recommended packet size is between 50 to 200 for high volume objects. Ensure you follow the below sequence
  • Code lists by using Asynchronous and scheduler-based communication
  • Then load the Organizational Data – Sales Orgs, Sales Offices, Sales Groups
  • Then start loading Business Partners, Account hierarchies, Employees, Products, Product Hierarchies, Registered products, Installed Base, Installation Points, Exchange Rates
  • After all master data is completed, then start loading transactional data such as Sales Quotes / Sales Orders / Service documents / Contracts etc
  • More detailed blog on Data Loading Best Practises from SAP S4HANA / S4HANA Cloud / ERP to Sales & Service Cloud V2 will be released soon.  Watch this space!! 

 

RATE LIMIT

What is Rate Limit

A rate limit is the maximum number of API calls a tenant, or user is allowed to make within a fixed period. It is enforced automatically by the platform to protect system stability and ensure fair usage

Why is Rate Limit Important

Rate limits are introduced to protect platform stability and regulate high volume data loads. Having rate limit can keep multi-tenant environments fair in shared infrastructure.

What type of calls are considered under rate limits

The calls from external systems – while consuming the REST APIs for third party integration or the calls from SAP S4HANA / ERP using prepackaged integration are currently RATE LIMITED.

What type of calls are not considered under rate limit

The calls from your connected groupware applications, mobile applications, V1 to V2 migration using Data Transition Tool are not Rate Limited

How to Calculate Rate Limit for your tenant

If you are loading the data into test tenants – 30000 API calls per day are allowed. For production tenants - “150000 + (No of licensed users * 1500)”  are allowed. After the limit is exhausted, the calls fail in the immediate source system (external application or SAP CPI) with HTTP 429 error messages.

How to monitor the API Usage

You can monitor the API Usage during data loading process – check the below link.
https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SVC/56436b4e8fa84dc8b4408c7795a012c4/07a150dce5534e6095d09c1e79430f41.html

As mentioned in the document, you can use your connected or embedded SAC to build a story based on the data source “Aggregated API Statistics” which updates the API usage, identify trends, and ensure fair use of resources to prevent overload and abuse

Requesting Temporary Rate Limit Enhancement

To increase the rate limit for certain number of days – create an incident from launchpad to CEC-CRM-CO-OPS with following information (Mandatory)

  • Either chose the correct tenant while creating the incident or mention the tenant URL
  • Approximate Data Volume in total (schedule of data loads each day)
  • Start Date & End Date of Data Loads
  • What are the different entities for which the data is loaded? Like Leads / Accounts / Opportunities / Sales Orders / Registered Products etc
  • API user details (REST API calls)

entry.title: My purchase requisition partial cancellation confirmation error
entry.content:
Logged in French

In "My purchase Requisition", a confirmation can be done. Usually it is possible to cancel/return the confirmation already done (respecting the status of the PR item).

Functionnal Steps

1. Logged in French language
2. Create a Purchase Requisition of 10 unit quantity : 'Pce'
3. Once approved, create the Purchase Order
4. Once done, confirm the Purchase Requisition item of 10Pce
5. In "my Purchase Requisition" , select the concerned purchase requisition, click on 'Return'
6. In the step of return, try to return a partial quantity, for example quantity of 6Pce

---> error

entry.title: Partner learning accelerator: Solution architect - SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Every great solution architecture usually starts the same way: a messy whiteboard, a few big business goals, and the question… how do we design this the right way?

If you’re working with SAP Business Technology Platform, you’ve probably been there.

That’s why we’re hosting the Partner learning accelerator: Solution architect – SAP BTP, a 90-minute live session designed for partners preparing for the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification.

We’ll break down what strong architecture really looks like across SAP BTP.  Not just theory. Real considerations: how to translate business requirements into technical blueprints, how integration strategies fit into enterprise landscapes, and where governance, DevOps, analytics, and master data play their role.

The goal is simple: help experienced partners connect the architectural dots faster and approach solution design with more clarity and confidence.

If you’re already building on SAP BTP and thinking about the next step in your architect journey… this session will be time well spent.

Register now and join the conversation.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/nov/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012265.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #EnterpriseArchitecture #CloudStrategy #PartnerEnablement

entry.title: Removing HTTP headers using PI web dispatcher
entry.content:
Hello everyone:

I have a client connecting to my PI server and sending the following HTTP headers, which apparently aren't accepted by my SOAP web service:

* Upgrade: h2c
* Connection: Upgrade, HTTP2-Settings

I'd like to know if anyone has encountered a similar situation and how they resolved it.

I've considered using the web dispatcher to suppress the headers that PI doesn't allow, which prevent the messages from even reaching the SOAP communication channel, but I don't know how to do it.

Thanks in advance for any help. Best regards.

entry.title: CreateTest User ID for S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

We are currently preparing for the Functional Integration Testing (FIT) phase in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Tenant and would like guidance on the recommended approach for setting up mass test users for manual testing.

Our requirement is to create multiple test user IDs in the test system and assign each of our custom business roles to dedicated test users. The objective is to test every custom role individually during the FIT phase to ensure that role authorizations, restrictions, and business catalogs are functioning as expected.

Specifically, we would like to understand:

What is the recommended approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Test Users for testing purposes?
Are there SAP best practices for setting up test users during the Functional Integration Testing phase?

Our goal is to have one or more dedicated test users per custom role so that each role can be validated independently during manual testing.

We would appreciate any documentation, recommended procedures, or tools that SAP suggests for managing this scenario efficiently.

Thanks,

Ravi


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CreateTest User ID for S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
To effectively set up test users for the Functional Integration Testing phase in SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition consider the following best practices

  1. Create Test Users using the Manage Workforce
  2. Once a worker is created use the Maintain Business Users app to automatically create a business user and Assign Business Roles to User
  3. Test Each Role Individually

Utilize SAP Cloud Identity Services for managing authentication and user access, Refer Set Up Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

Refer documentation on Create a Business User in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal  for detailed steps

entry.title: Accessibility - Dropdown lists
entry.content:
After an accessibility audit on SAP SuccessFactors, here are some non-compliances that were noted regarding dropdownlist, that we would appreciate to see evolve:

  • Up/Down arrow keys: navigate between list options

  • Space bar: select an option

  • The tabindex=1 attribute should not be used on interactive elements except in specific cases (which must be communicated to users for proper functionality).

entry.title: Re: Emarsys contact deduplication
entry.content:
Hello Joyca, 

thank you for your question and apologies for the delay. Hopefully this helps answer your question. 

Emarsys has a page to manage duplicate suspects: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EMARSYS/f8e2fafeea804018a954a8857d9dfff3/fdf598a674c11014adc1d1efe5e1d939.html#managing-duplicate-suspects

However, we rely on customers to select the correct identifier upon import to avoid creating duplicate contacts.

The deduplication handling will leverage the Unique Identifier field selected as the key_id in API or the Unique Identifier field in the CSV import to ensure it does not create a duplicate contact with the same value for that Unique Identifier.

Additionally, here are some other resources:

  • a video explaining Data Requirements and Contact Identification.
  • This course could also be useful, specifically Unit 2, Lesson 2 

Please let us know if you have any further questions and thank you so much for attending our live sessions! 

Jessica Becker

entry.title: Handling Conditional Key Figure Disaggregation in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hi SAP IBP Community,

I’m working on a scenario in SAP IBP where I need to disaggregate a key figure. The challenge is that I never want the disaggregation to land on products with a specific material type (Material Type X).

Currently, the disaggregation is based on another key figure, which works fine when that reference key figure has values. However, problems occur when the reference key figure is empty or zero, because standard disaggregation logic falls back to an equal split.

We also cannot simply work with ones and zeros to control this, because our adjustments happen on two levels: MTHPRODLOCCUST. Sometimes we want to make adjustments at the location level, and sometimes at the customer level. Using ones or zeros can unintentionally create values (“1s”) in places where we don’t want them.

Even using a formula like:

IF("MATTYPEID" = 'X', NULL, IF(ISNULL(REFERENCE_KF, 0, REFERENCE_KF))

doesn’t solve the problem, because if IBP sees a 0 or NULL, it still falls back to an equal split. This makes it tricky to prevent allocations to unwanted materials while maintaining the correct disaggregation logic across multiple levels.

My question:
How can I configure disaggregation in SAP IBP so that:

  • It never applies to products of Material Type X.

  • It uses the reference key figure when available.

  • It avoids fallback to equal split or unintended ones/zeros across multiple levels.

Any guidance, best practices, or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Understanding and choosing the appropriate Valuated SIT
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s dynamic business environment, stock transfers between plants—whether within the same company code or across different codes—are a common practice. These movements are not just logistical necessities; they are strategic decisions aimed at optimizing operations, reducing excess inventory, and accelerating the alignment of costs with revenues.

Global B2B operations often involve affiliated or related companies, where the transfer of goods plays a crucial role in balancing supply and demand. Typically, high-value inventory is prioritized for such transfers, especially when the cost of shipping is significantly lower than the value of the goods themselves and when the shipping happens through multiple modes of transit across countries. Yet, one critical question always arises: who bears responsibility for the stock while it is in transit?

This concern lies at the heart of valuated stock in transit in SAP—a functionality designed to provide clarity, accountability, and financial accuracy during these crucial movements.

Solution options in SAP :

Valuated stock in transit is available in the following intra-company-code and cross-company-code processes.

1. Stock transfer with transfer of title at goods issue.

Stock transfer to valuated stock in transit of the receiving plant during goods issue by the supplying plant.

When to use -

When goods are shipped between different company codes, especially across countries, ownership often needs to transfer at the point of shipment.

This ensures that the selling company recognizes revenue, and the buying company recognizes the liability immediately.

Common in international trade under Incoterms like FOB (Free on Board) or EXW (Ex Works). Certain contracts stipulate that the buyer assumes responsibility once goods leave the seller’s premises.

Simplifies logistics when the receiving plant is far away or delivery times are long.

Avoids delays in financial postings since the transfer is recorded at goods issue.

STO Document Type: NBCR

Delivery type: NCCR

Item category: NCCR

Schedule line category: N1

Assigning schedule line category to item category: NCCR-N1

Defining Proof of delivery: NO

During VL02n – Material is transferred to Unrestricted stock of issuing plant to valuated stock in transit of receiving plant. – Transfer of title is completed.

During MIGO – Material is transferred from Valuated stock in transit of receiving plant to unrestricted stock of receiving plant using movement type 109.

Stock transfer is thereby completed.

2. Stock transfer with transfer of title at goods receipt.

Stock is transferred from valuated stock in transit of the supplying plant to directly to the unrestricted use stock of the receiving plant.

When to use-

Suitable when goods are shipped within the same region or country, where transit times are short and risks are minimal.

The issuing plant continues to bear responsibility until the receiving plant confirms receipt.

Certain agreements specify that ownership transfers only upon delivery.

Common under Incoterms like DAP (Delivered at Place) or DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), where the seller is responsible until goods reach the buyer’s location.

When the company wants the issuing plant to remain accountable for goods until they are physically received.

Helps avoid disputes if goods are damaged or lost in transit.

STO Document Type: NBC2

Delivery type: NCC2

Item category: NCC2

Schedule line category: N2

Assigning schedule line category to item category: NBC2-N2

Defining Proof of delivery: YES

During VL02n – Material is transferred to Unrestricted stock of issuing plant to valuated stock in transit of issuing plant.

During VLPOD – When the POD is confirmed, the stock is moved from the stock in transit of the issuing plant to the unrestricted use stock of the receiving plant. – Transfer of title is completed here, and the stock transfer ends thereby.

3.Stock transfer with transfer of title during transit.

This scenario in SAP is a middle ground between title transfer at goods issue and goods receipt. Here, ownership of the goods changes hands while the stock is physically in transit, at a defined point between shipment and delivery.

When to use –

This scenario in SAP is a middle ground between title transfer at goods issue and goods receipt. Here, ownership of the goods changes hands while the stock is physically in transit, at a defined point between shipment and delivery.

Common under Incoterms like CFR (Cost and Freight) or CIF (Cost, Insurance, Freight), where ownership transfers once goods cross a certain point (e.g., loaded on a vessel).

The buyer assumes ownership mid-transit, but the seller may still cover certain costs.

When goods are valuable and transit takes weeks (e.g., overseas shipping), companies may prefer to transfer title mid-transit to reflect the economic reality.

Allows both parties to recognize revenue and costs in the correct accounting period, especially when shipment and receipt fall in different months or quarters.

STO Document Type: NBC3

Delivery type: NCC3

Item category: NCC3

Schedule line category: N3

Assigning schedule line category to item category: NCC3-N3

Defining Proof of delivery: YES

During VL02n – Material is transferred to Unrestricted stock of issuing plant to valuated stock in transit of issuing plant.

During VLPOD – When the POD is confirmed, the stock is moved from the stock in transit of the issuing plant to the valuated stock in transit of the receiving plant. – Transfer of title happens here.

During MIGO – The movement type 109 is used, the stock is posted from valuated stock in transit of the receiving plant to the unrestricted use stock of the receiving plant. Thereby, completing the stock transfer.

Conclusion:

SAP provides multiple options for handling stock transfers—whether title passes at goods issue, goods receipt, or during transit. This flexibility is intentional, allowing businesses to align system behaviour with their accounting principles, contractual obligations, and the terms agreed between company codes.

However, the effectiveness of these options depends on clarity. A well-defined set of rules, agreed upon in advance, is essential before configuring the system. Without this, companies risk inconsistencies in ownership, valuation, and financial reporting.

By carefully analyzing all the impacting factors and setting clear rules upfront, organizations can ensure that SAP’s stock transfer functionality not only supports logistics but also strengthens financial accuracy and compliance.

entry.title: SAP Excel Error - Line, Data, Record breaking
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

Hope you are doing well. This blog is regarding Incorrect Data downloaded in Excel. When you open the file you will observe 1 thing that many of the records are breatking and moved to Next Line.

For e.g. see below screenshot. 2nd line is breaking and 3rd line is created. This is the huge problem when you have large data.

Now if there are 50000 records per month and 2000 and more records are breaking what will you do?

Before proceeding further let us understand few things which are important.

- Identification of Data

When you get this kind of issue which are very critical for some uploads in interface, you get panic and nervous. Nothing to worry.  Firtst tihng identify the records which are breaking. Idenfity the date for which this records is breaking. For .e.g. you are executing report for March 2026, there are 50000 records. but record which is breaking is on 15th march onwards. So first identfy the data. Make it a Sample data.

- Execution of Sample data to identify the root cause.

Execute the report again for the sample data. Verify the result if data are breaking or not If it is breaking than this is confimed that data are breaking collectively and also individually. Mean there is the problem in Data and not report.

- Debugging of data (Option 1)

Connect with technical team and request them to debug this data which are breaking to find out the root cause.

- Why Data are breaking

In our case data was breaking because there was # in the Purchase order header text. Due to this # data were breaking and were presented in another line. This was identified by our technical team.  Now there is a trick here.

- In purchase order (ME23N) you willl see no # symbol in text. For e.g. 'Purchae from Local Depot' is the text. This is header text. Do you see any # here? No. So where is the # tag?

- Now go to Table EKKO and check that Header text field. Do you find that #? No. My god, so where is it coming from. Here is the most important thing.

- Now technical team will debug this PO header text and while debugging # will be reflected. Surprising. This is reflected in debugging but not in table nor in ME23N.  So here is the root cause.

Debug all data and you will find many other symbols which will be breaking data. 

- But where the # is coming?

We created the PO and we found no issue. No # was generated. We posted multiple times and found that 1 of them was having #. We realized that after putting header text, we pressed Enter button. And after this Enter, # was getting generated in back ground. And you know how much time we invested to know this, nearly 2 months. Yes 2 months. 

So pressing "ENTER" button twice created this issue.

- Solution

Our technical team member was very intelligent and crafted intelligent solution. A Super Woman. She created 1 set in GS01 and started putting all symbols found in debugging. She put some coding and post that all Data were getting downloaded correctly. This was amazing.

We wrote to SAP also and they were also analyzing the issue, but before that we found this solution and still it in place. 

So if you face this problem in future. Follow above steps and this problem can be solved in 1 or 2 days. 

In another project we received same issue and we followed above approach. It was resoled.

So all the best.

I hope you liked my Blog.

Regards

Zunaid Hingora

entry.title: SAP EWM Transaction /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG
entry.content:
SAP EWM Transaction Report: /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG

Summary

  • The /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG transaction enables automated modification of stock attributes in SAP Extended Warehouse Management without physical stock movement
  • Key capabilities include stock type changes, inspection document creation, and consignment stock conversion with comprehensive background processing support
  • Primary use cases encompass quality management processes, expired inventory handling, and automated consignment stock management

Transaction Overview

Transaction Code

  • Primary: /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG (Change Stock Attributes)
  • Alternative: Program /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE via SE38
  • Menu Access: SAP Easy Access → Logistics → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Change Stock Attributes

 

Business Purpose

  • Core business objective: Automate stock attribute modifications on one-off or recurring basis to support warehouse operations and compliance requirements
  • Functional area coverage: Extended Warehouse Management with integration into Quality Management and ERP systems
  • Integration points: SAP ERP for goods movements, Quality Management for inspection processes, and Financial Accounting for inventory valuation

System Requirements

  • SAP Version: S/4HANA only (available since SAP S/4HANA 2020 FPS01)
  • Required Modules: SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), optional Quality Management (QM) integration
  • Prerequisites: EWM warehouse configuration, appropriate authorization objects, and tolerance group assignments

Functional Capabilities

Core Features

  1. Stock Type Modification: Change stock types (e.g., F2 to Q4, F2 to B6) without physical movement
  2. Inspection Document Creation: Generate quality inspection documents with IOT 5 (Q-Inspection Product/Batch Whse-Internal)
  3. Consignment Stock Conversion: Automatically post consignment stock to own stock after specified time periods

Processing Options

  • Stock Type Change Mode: Modify stock attributes while maintaining physical location
  • Inspection Creation Mode: Create inspection documents and post to QM stock types
  • Consignment Posting Mode: Convert vendor consignment stock to company-owned inventory

Selection Parameters

Parameter

Description

Required

Notes

Warehouse Number

EWM warehouse identifier

Yes

Defines processing scope

Stock Type From

Source stock type

Conditional

Required for stock type changes

Stock Type To

Destination stock type

Conditional

Required for stock type changes

Material

Material number or range

No

Enables selective processing

Storage Location

ERP storage location

No

Additional filtering criteria

Movement Reason

Business reason code

Conditional

Mandatory for certain modes

Date Range

Selection by goods receipt/inspection dates

No

Time-based filtering

Navigation and Access

Menu Path

  • SAP Easy Access → Logistics → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Change Stock Attributes

Alternative Access Methods

  • Direct Transaction: /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG
  • Program Execution: SE38 → /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE
  • Background Processing: Schedule as background job for automated execution

Configuration Requirements

Authorization Objects

  • /SCWM/TOCR: Warehouse task creation authorization with Warehouse Process Type restrictions
  • Tolerance Group Assignment: Background users must be assigned via Extended Warehouse Management → Settings → Physical Inventory → Assign User to Tolerance Group for Difference Analyzer

Master Data Prerequisites

  • Inspection Rules: IOT 5 (Q-Inspection Product/Batch Whse-Internal) configuration for quality management scenarios
  • Storage Type Configuration: Mixed owner settings for consignment stock processing
  • Material Master: Complete material master data with appropriate stock management settings

System Configuration

  • Warehouse Process Type: Define whether warehouse tasks are created automatically during posting changes
  • Default Values: Configure destination data for stocks in conjunction with warehouse process types

Step-by-Step Usage Guide

Preparation Steps

  1. Verify tolerance group assignment for background processing users
  2. Ensure inspection rules are configured for quality management scenarios
  3. Validate storage type settings for mixed owner scenarios

Execution Process

  1. Access Transaction: Navigate to /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG or execute program /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE
  2. Configure Selection Parameters: Enter warehouse number, stock types, materials, and date ranges
  3. Choose Processing Mode: Select stock type change, inspection creation, or consignment posting
  4. Executed Report: Run in foreground for testing or schedule as background job for automation

Post-Processing Activities

  • Review Logs: Analyze detailed processing results and error messages
  • Monitor Warehouse Tasks: Track generated warehouse tasks for physical movements
  • Verify Integration: Confirm ERP posting and quality management document creation

Output and Results

Report on Output Format

  • Detailed logs showing successful stock type changes, created inspection documents, and warehouse tasks
  • Comprehensive tracking of processed items with original and new stock attributes
  • Error reporting for failed processing with specific failure reasons

Generated Documents

  • Warehouse Tasks: For physical movements when required
  • Inspection Documents: Quality management inspection lots
  • ERP Material Documents: Goods movement postings for inventory updates

System Updates

  • Stock Attribute Changes: Updated stock types, inspection status, and ownership
  • ERP Integration: Synchronized inventory values and stock classifications

Business Scenarios

Use Case 1: Consignment Stock Conversion

  • Objective: Automatically convert vendor consignment stock to own stock after 90 days
  • Process: Schedule background job to identify consignment stock older than 90 days and convert to own stock
  • Outcome: Reduced manual intervention and improved cash flow management

Use Case 2: Quality Management Integration

  • Objective: Create inspection documents for batch-managed materials requiring quality control
  • Process: Post unrestricted stock to quality inspection stock and generate inspection lots
  • Outcome: Systematic quality control with full traceability and compliance

Use Case 3: Expired Inventory Management

  • Objective: Block stock with expired shelf-life expiration dates
  • Process: Automatically convert unrestricted stock to blocked stock based on expiration criteria
  • Outcome: Prevention of non-compliant product distribution and regulatory compliance

Integration Points

ERP Integration

  • Goods Movement Posting: Automatic creation of material documents in ERP for inventory updates
  • Financial Integration: Synchronization with Financial Accounting for inventory valuation

Related SAP Modules

  • Quality Management (QM): Integration for inspection lot creation and quality stock management
  • Materials Management (MM): Stock type changes reflected in inventory management

External System Interfaces

  • Warehouse Management Systems: Integration with external WMS for physical execution
  • Quality Systems: Connection to external quality management platforms

Troubleshooting Guide

Common Issues

Issue

Symptoms

Root Cause

Resolution

Complete Processing Failure

All items fail to process

Single line with errors (e.g., obsolete material status)

Validate all materials before execution to prevent all-or-nothing failure

Performance Issues

Extended processing times

Large dataset processing

Break into smaller batches or schedule during off-peak hours

XML Processing Errors

Special character handling failures

Bin names contain special characters like "/$"

Implement BAdIs QIE_ELEMENT_PROP_CONVERT/QIE_INSP_DOC_PROP_CONVERT

Error Messages

  • Error Code: Material Status Obsolete
    • Description: Cannot process materials with obsolete status
    • Resolution: Update material master data or exclude obsolete materials from selection

Performance Considerations

  • Dataset Size: Large datasets can cause extended processing times requiring batch processing
  • Background Processing: Use background jobs for recurring operations to avoid system performance impact
  • Tolerance Group Configuration: Proper tolerance group assignment prevents authorization issues

Best Practices

Operational Guidelines

  • Data Validation: Always validate master data completeness before execution to prevent processing failures
  • Batch Processing: Use smaller batches for large datasets to improve performance and error handling
  • Background Scheduling: Schedule recurring processes during off-peak hours to minimize system impact

Security Considerations

  • Authorization Management: Implement proper authorization controls using /SCWM/TOCR and warehouse process type restrictions
  • Tolerance Group Assignment: Ensure appropriate tolerance group assignments for different handling

Data Management

  • Master Data Integrity: Maintain complete and accurate master data to prevent processing errors
  • Configuration Consistency: Ensure consistent configuration across warehouse process types and storage types

Related Transactions

Transaction

Description

Relationship

/SCWM/POST

Posting Change for Warehouse Number

Direct posting change functionality

/SCWM/MON

Warehouse Monitor

Stock monitoring and manual attribute changes

/SCWM/ERP_STOCKCHECK

ERP Stock Check

Inventory synchronization between EWM and ERP

Technical Specifications

Program Details

  • Program Name: /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE
  • Package: SAP Extended Warehouse Management
  • Object Type: Report program with background processing capabilities

Database Tables

  • Stock Tables: EWM stock management tables for attribute updates
  • Warehouse Task Tables: Generated warehouse task records
  • Integration Tables: ERP interface tables for goods movement posting

Enhancement Points

  • BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_STOCK: Custom authority checks for stock posting
  • Quality Management BAdIs: QIE_ELEMENT_PROP_CONVERT and QIE_INSP_DOC_PROP_CONVERT for special character handling

Conclusion and Recommendations

Key Benefits

  • Process Automation: Eliminates manual stock attribute changes, reducing errors and processing time
  • Compliance Support: Ensures regulatory compliance through automated quality management and expiration handling
  • Integration Excellence: Seamless integration with ERP and Quality Management systems

Hope that can help you.

Thank you and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: CP Merge - How to undo a wrongly assigned CP merge?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have 2 employees who have a CP merge. They have ironically the same surname, and were born on the same date, in the same country, and were therefore in our system automatically merged under one Central Person number although they are actually 2 different persons! They do in fact have unique middle names.

Usually I delete the IT0031 record from the falsely merged employees and HRP1000 and HRP1001 update automatically. In this case however, neither of the employees has an IT0031 record. 

I cannot find any guidelines on how to safely remove a wrongly assigned CP merge. All help is greatly appreciated.

Thank you and kind regards

Warren

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
Re: Questions about the file attachment feature in SAP Bu...
entry.content:
Hello HIROKI_TAKASHIMA,

If you need to enable file attachments within your SAP Build Process Automation workflow, you can achieve this by using Forms instead of trying to insert a User Task directly.

Option 1: Use a Submit Form

A Submit Form allows end users to provide input to the process — and it supports file uploads.
You can use this at the start of the process to collect all required files.

Option 2: Use an Approval Form

If your scenario requires approvals, the Approval Form also allows attaching or viewing uploaded files.
This can be used at any review or approval step in the process.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: “Custom attributes” in ABAP platform workflows and related inboxes
entry.content:
SAP Business Workflow was first introduced in 1995 as part of SAP R/3 Release 3.0 (see: SAP R/3: Architecture, Function, Implementation" by Buck-Emden et.al..)

This longevity speaks to the robustness and deep integration of the tool in the ABAP platform – why also often the name ABAP platform workflow is used. A main goal was and is to distribute workitems (often also called “to dos” or “tasks” – don´t mix up this runtime artefact with the task-object from design time [e.g. TS30000016]) to the next worker to accomplish business outcomes in a coordinated way and as effortless as possible for human workers interacting with a digital system. Same is true for compliance approval processes implemented via flexible workflows in SAP S/4HANA CLOUD - we celebrate 10th Birthday this year.

Almost from the beginning, customizable task attributes and personalized worklist searching & sorting have been essential for enabling end-users to complete their work with freedom and flexibility—allowing them to work in the sequence their company or business process requires. This directly addresses all three psychological needs that Self-Determination Theory identifies as drivers of intrinsic motivation and self-actualization: autonomy, competence, and social relatedness. The concept of “custom attributes”: Company and business process-specific metadata that enables:

  • Task filtering and prioritization
  • Personalized worklist management
  • Context-aware task processing

This blog post deals mainly with the latest technology to define CDS Entity based custom attributes (which is not covered in the workflow overview training BIT602). For the sake of completeness, the older options are also mentioned or linked.

31 and also 10 years are tremendous for software components and tools. Congratulations to all contributed to that amazing story.

CDS Entity based custom attributes for My Inbox and SAP Task Center

CDS Entity-based custom attributes are the recommended approach for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and available sind ABAP Platform 2023. The CDS Entity must have identical key fields as the leading object associated with the workflow task or scenario. This method is supported for flexible workflow scenarios and their steps only.

Prerequisites Checklist

✓ Flexible workflow scenario with attached leading object

✓ CDS Entity key fields match leading object keys

✓ CDS Entity has @Workflow: { enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ] } * key user possible [see section "Custom Attributes via key user extensibility" below]

✓ For amount/quantity fields: annotations include @Semantics

✓ Maximum 30 attributes per step (Task Center limitation)

Development

Note that it will only be possible to use the CDS based configuration for Tasks or Scenarios with an attached leading object.

At runtime, the system uses the CDS Entity name and field to populate custom attribute values. Configuration varies by deployment model:

  • SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF: Application development
  • SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST: Partner/OP customer development
  • SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS: Cloud customers (via SSCUI)

Note that the chosen CDS Entity has to have the annotation @Workflow: { enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ] } if used in the transactions SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF, SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST or SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS. 

If the CDS entity field chosen is an amount or value field and carries one of the following annotations '@Semantics.amount.currencyCode' or '@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure' the corresponding currency or unit information will be additionally exposed to the frontend. The label of the corresponding column would in this case be taken from the '@EndUserText.label' given in the CDS Entity'.

Note that the annotation referencing the currency or unit of measure is evaluated during definition of custom attribute. This means that if any changes are done to those annotations in the CDS view after the custom attribute was maintained these changes will not be evaluated anymore. If such changes are done the custom attribute should be removed and recreated.

Custom Attributes Definitions created in SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF are not displayed in the transaction SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS but can be redefined using the button.

Definition and customization

SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition provides three transactions for viewing, defining, and modifying CDS-based custom attributes:

Custom Attributes: Definition and Customization

Transaction Types

  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF

 Purpose: Define custom attributes across all clients

  • Use Case: Typically used for solution delivery (e.g., by SAP Partners)
  • Transport Method: Workbench request
  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST

Purpose: Define custom attributes for a specific client

  • Capabilities:
    • Define new custom attributes
    • Overwrite definitions from SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF
  • Transport Method: Customizing transport
  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS

Purpose: Enable cloud customers to define or modify custom attributes

  • Availability:
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition (full transaction access)
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition (via SSCUI - CDS view definition only) available from 2402 onwards over "Configure Your Solution"
  • Key Features:
    • CDS-based definition capability
    • "Convert into CDS based cust. Attribute" button to migrate from task container definitions
    • "Copy Entry from System Settings" button to replicate existing definitions

Important Considerations

Aspect

Details

CDS Definition Restrictions

Only allowed if no task container definition exists

Conversion Impact

Converting from Expression to CDS removes the original definition and cannot be reverted by cloud customers

Already Redefined Attributes

Do not appear in the selection screen when copying

Maximum Limit

30 custom attributes per workflow step (Task Center service limitation)

Recommendation

Define maximum 30 custom attributes per step

Granularity Options

Task Level

  • Expose the same custom attributes across all workflow steps using that task
  • Example: Task TS78500059

Workflow Step Level

  • Expose custom attributes for a specific workflow step (identified by Step ID)
  • Example: Task WS02000006, Step ID 1
  • Benefit: Useful when a task is reused differently (e.g., different approval perspectives)

Configuration Fields

Field

Description

Notes

Use Case

Groups or filters user attributes

Free text (max. 20 characters)

Name of Attribute

Unique identifier

Special identifiers starting with "custom" display in specific inbox locations

Description

Identifying text

Max. 40 characters; translatable via ABAP translation mechanism

Rank

Priority/importance indicator

Higher rank = higher importance; affects display order in Task Center

CDS Entity

Source data entity name

(CDS-based only) Must have same key as the task's leading object

CDS Entity Field

Specific field containing display data

(CDS-based only)

Expression

Container element reference

(Container-based only) Use format: &ELEMENT_NAME&; supports combined values

Type

ABAP data type

(Container-based only)

Field

External field type

(Container-based only)

Screenshots

 

 

Custom Attributes via key user extensibility 

WIth SAP S/4HANA Cloud , public edition 2505 and 2025 private edition key users can define custom attributes through the SSCUI in the Manage Your Solution app. If additional CDS Views are needed as a basis for their definition, you can use App Custom CDS Views to generate a CDS View by selecting the Custom CDS Scenario "Custom Attributes for Task." See detailed documentation. Note that CDS views generated this way will appear in the value help within the SSCUI. However, you must ensure that the key fields of the generated CDS view match the key fields of the workflow task's or workflow scenario's leading object. Additionally, the transport containing the generated CDS view should be released either before or together with the transport containing the SSCUI configuration.

Special Attributes in Custom Attributes Configuration

This part explains how to create complex custom attributes (with relations available with ABAP Platform Cloud 2402 /ABAP Platform 2025) beyond simple transaction-maintained ones:

Three Types of Special Attributes

Type

Annotation

Use Case

Value + Unit of Measure

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure

Quantities with UOM (e.g., 10 KG)

Value + Currency

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode

Monetary amounts with currency (e.g., 100 USD)

Value + Description

@ObjectModel.text.element

Human-readable text pairs (e.g., Code + Description)

Please note, these related attributes are not available for My Inbox.

Key Requirements

CDS Entity Configuration

  • All referenced fields must be in the same CDS Entity, OR
  • Fields must be connected via an association with a single "on" condition

Text Arrangement (for Value + Description available from ABAP Platform Cloud 2405 / ABAP Platform 2025)

  • Use @ui.textArrangement: annotation to define display order
  • Default behavior: #TEXT_FIRST (description shown before value)

Language Considerations

Two Categories:

  1. Language Dependent
    • Example: itemCurrency, itemUOM
    • Evaluated per user's logon language
  2. Language Independent
    • Example: documentType, documentCategory
    • Same value across all languages

Language Management

  • Configuration Table: SWFCLANG (view: SWFCLANG_V)
  • User for Evaluation: SAP_WFRT logon language
  • Maintenance:
    • Private Cloud: SM30 transaction
    • Public Cloud: Configure Your Solution application
  • Storage: All texts stored with language keys per translation vector

 

Transport

Annotations and attribute configurations are transported together in the same transport request.

Sample CDS Entity and Custom Attributes

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'WF Demo: Leave Request Base (CDS)'

@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #CDS_MODELING_ASSOCIATION_TARGET ]

@Workflow.enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ]

define root view entity ZDK_WORKFLOWLEAVEREQUEST2
  as select from swxformabs

  association [0..1] to ZDK_DEMO_I_LR_TYPETEXT as _Text     on $projection.holidaytp1 = _Text.Type and _Text.Language = 'E'
  association [0..*] to I_Currency             as _Currency on $projection.Currency = _Currency.Currency

{
  key swxformabs.formnumber  as WorkflowLeaveRequest,

      swxformabs.approvby    as WFLeaveApprovedBy,
      swxformabs.createdby   as WFCreatedBy,

      swxformabs.addrnumber  as WFCreatorAddressNumber,
      swxformabs.pernr       as WFCreatorPersonalNumber,
      swxformabs.name        as WFCreatorNameText,
      swxformabs.department  as WFCreatorDepartment,
      swxformabs.costcenter  as WFCreatorCostcenter,

      swxformabs.firstday1   as WFLeavePartOneFirstDay,
      swxformabs.lastday1    as WFLeavePartOneLastDay,

      @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_Text'
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ '_Text.TypeText' ]
      swxformabs.holidaytp1,
      @EndUserText.label: 'LeaveRequest Type'
      _Text.TypeText         as LeaveType,
      swxformabs.reason      as WFLeaveReason,
      swxformabs.contact     as WFLeaveContact,
      swxformabs.procstate   as WFLeaveProcessState,

      case swxformabs.procstate
        when 'A' then 'Approved'
        when 'R' then 'Rejected'
        else 'New'
      end                    as WFLeaveProcessStateText,

      @EndUserText.label: 'LeaveRequest Currency'
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ '_Currency._Text.CurrencyName' ]
      @UI.textArrangement: #TEXT_LAST
      case when swxformabs.holidaytp1 = 'R' then 'EUR'
           else 'USD'
      end                    as Currency,

      _Currency,
      _Text
}

 

Custom Attribute Types and Database Storage

Virtual Element-Based Custom Attributes

These attributes derive values from virtual CDS elements. The database record stores an exit-class name in the EXIT_CLASS field, which is used to calculate the attribute values.

Text Reference Custom Attributes

This configuration uses two database entries:

  • A primary custom attribute with REF_ATTRIBUTE_NAME pointing to a text reference attribute
  • A text reference custom attribute that provides the actual values
Currency-Based Custom Attributes

This structure requires three database entries arranged hierarchically:

  • Primary custom attribute (references a currency attribute)
  • Currency reference attribute (references a text attribute)
  • Text reference attribute (base values)
Unit of Measure Custom Attributes

Similar to currency attributes, this configuration also uses three entries:

Primary custom attribute (references a unit of measure attribute)

Unit of measure reference attribute (references a text attribute)

Text reference attribute (base values)

Implementation Considerations
Virtual CDS Elements

While virtual CDS elements are technically supported for custom attribute definitions, their use is not recommended for performance and maintenance reasons.

Runtime Behavior
Data Availability and Timing

Custom attribute values are calculated and stored when work items are created. However, not all container elements may be available at creation time, and CDS entity data retrieved at this point may differ from real-time values. Post-execution values and UI-updated attributes may not exist during creation, making them unsuitable for inbox display.

Dynamic Updates

Developers can update (available since ABAP Platform Cloud 2505 / ABAP Platform 2025) custom attributes post-creation using the CL_SWF_UTL_RUN_USR_ATTR_CHG utility class. After populating a local buffer with work items (via append_business_obj, append_workflow, or append_workitems), calling request_custom_attribute_chg asynchronously updates all non-final work items within that workflow

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( )
lo_util->append_workflow( '<workflow id>' ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

or business object.

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( ).

lo_util->append_business_obj( VALUE #( catid  = '<category_id>'
                                       typeid = '<type_id>'
                                       instid = '<business_object_instance_id>' ) ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

Performance recommendations

  • Include texts in CDS entities: Text fields should be incorporated directly into CDS entities used for customer attribute definitions (as shown in the example above). This should be documented.
  • Minimize CDS entities: Keep the number of CDS entities used for customer attribute definitions as small as possible—ideally just one. This should be clearly documented.
  • Push domain and reference table texts to database level: If texts from domain values or reference tables are needed, associate them at the database level by linking text associations to the CDS entity that collects these texts. Refer to `I_WorkflowStatusText` as a reference example.
  • Enable full buffering for text tables: Text tables should be configured for full buffering whenever possible.
  • Avoid CDS Virtual Elements: Virtual elements should not be used for customer attribute definitions. If a virtual element must be used, implement the `GET_CALCULATION_INFO` method to specify which CDS entity elements are required for data calculation.

My inbox

How custom attributes are displayed and consumed depends on the view of My Inbox that is being used.

Master-Detail View

The Master-Detail View is the standard view of My Inbox in ABAP Platform. See further details here: Master-Detail View.

When using some predefined names in the definition of custom attributes, some labels in the My Inbox UI can be replaced with the exposed data.

You can find further details in this documentation: Display Custom Attributes in the List View.

Screenshot.

Since ABAP Platform 2102 and ABAP Platform 2021, when performing a mass action (decision), custom attributes are exposed to the user in the multi-selection view. The table in the multi-selection view can be sorted by the custom attributes and also be personalized.

Screenshot

Expert View

The Expert View offers a tabular representation of the standard and the exposed custom attributes of the workflow tasks. The attributes are displayed as columns that can be personalized, sorted, grouped and filtered.

See further details here: Expert View.

SAP Task Center

For tasks to be available in SAP Task Center via the integration also, the defined custom attributes will be exposed to Task Center and be persisted together with the standard attributes in the cache of the Task Center Service on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

The cache of SAP Task Center task is then used by the UIs (e.g. the Web App and SAP Mobile Start) without having to additionally call the task providers. How the custom attributes are shown depends on the connected UI. 

Container based custom attributes

The second option defining custom attributes is to use a reference to particular elements of the workflow container.
Once the task is created they are stored as name-value-pair in the database. As opposed to the container, which is commonly stored as XML, custom attributes can be read via a simple database select.

Following elements can be used:

  • Plain elements
  • Table of plain elements
  • Object attributes (which are plain elements or a table of plain elements) 

Structure elements are not supported. Only simple fields of the structure can be used. The supported data types are:

C

Character string

N

Character string with digits only

D

Date (YYYYMMDD)

T

Time (HHMMSS)

I

Integer number (4-byte integer with sign)

b

1-byte integer, whole number <= 254

s

2-byte integer, only for length field before LCHR or LRAW

P

Packed number

F

Floating point number to accuracy of 8 bytes

g

Character string with variable length (ABAP type STRING)

 

How custom attributes can be defined depends on the UI in which they should be shown.

Please check these two blogs for more details:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/embellishing-your-approvals-without-coding-my-inbox/ba-p/13323357

2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-fiori-my-inbox-2-0-sp02-features/ba-p/13228953

Exit based custom attributes for My Inbox via Task Gateway Configuration

In general the definition of custom attributes via the configuration in SAP Business Workflow should be preferred to the configuration within Task Gateway since:

Activating a BAdI implementation for a task definition invalidates the definitions and customizations that were done for that task definition in SAP Business Workflow. By that preventing extensions by customers.

Custom attributes that were defined by means of this BAdI will not be exposed to Task Center.

While strongly discouraged custom attributes can still provided at task instance level by implementing the /IWWRK/BADI_TGW_CUSTOM_ATTR BAdI in the /IWWRK/ES_TGW_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE enhancement spot. You can find more information here: Custom Attributes. 

 

History: dynamic columns in SAP Business Workplace

For Business Workflow it is possible to create custom columns.

Up to 12 custom columns can be defined using the transaction SWL1 or the menu path  Tools → Business Workflow → Development → Definition Tools → Worklist Client → Dynamic Columns for Worklist.

the following fields can be edited:

  1. User Name: Relevant user name or ‘*’ to support all users
  2. Task : Relevant task type
  3. Expression: Value that should be displayed in the UI.  As source a container element should be specified via its name enclosed into the ‘&’ character. Please ensure that the container element name is taken from the relevant task type, for example '&NAME&'
  4. Header: Label that should be displayed in the UI.

 

Please check these blogs:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sbwp-customization-tip-for-inbox-customization/ba-p/13304850

2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/display-dynamic-columns-for-business-workplace-in-the-uwl/ba-p/1288610

Thanks to all contributors to this features & blog.

entry.title: Re: Sales Force To SAP Business Partner Intergration
entry.content:
Dear @Ashish13,

Yes this integration is possible in Public Cloud using the standard Business Partner integration approach. The main starting point on the SAP side is communication scenario SAP_COM_0008 and the standard interfaces include API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (OData) and Business Partner SOAP replication. SAP can generate the Business Partner number internally, while external Salesforce customer/address identifiers should be handled as external reference values rather than SAP internal keys

High-level guide where to start:

  1. First align which data must be created in SAP Business Partner general data, customer company code data, customer sales area data, partner functions, and address-related data
  2. Choose whether Salesforce will send data to SAP using API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (OData) or the SOAP-based Business Partner replication service under the standard BP integration scenario
  3. In Public Cloud, create the required communication setup for SAP_COM_0008 using Communication Management and the relevant communication arrangement
  4. Map Salesforce customer data to the SAP Business Partner/customer model, including general data, company code, sales area, and address-related structures
  5. Decide that SAP will create the internal BP number through internal grouping, while Salesforce identifiers are stored as external identifiers for traceability and updates
  6. Confirm whether bill-to and ship-to should be modeled as separate partner-role assignments, separate business partners or multiple addresses depending on the business design
  7. Start with create and update scenarios for one customer, then test company code extension, sales area extension, address updates and bill-to/ship-to behavior
  8. Validate that the customer created in Salesforce is correctly replicated into SAP with the expected BP number, roles, organizational data and address handling

More details below:

  • Business Partner Integration

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/f7399c7374d3475a96a56a0856b5f3ee.html

  • Business Partner (A2X)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/85043858ea0f9244e10000000a4450e5.html

  • Data Replication Using SOAP

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/ba5eb8a54eef4822b3de4e2958f32554.html

  • Business Partner Number Grouping

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/15c19fb1f02544eebafaf5953a8e14ef.html

  • Multiple Address Handling in Customer Master Data

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/f3289df37b79410590db2eca6e5fe6bc.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Access CDS View data through SAP Integration Suite fo...
entry.content: Hi @qiangwan87, Thank you very much for your detailed explanation and suggestions — I really appreciate the time you took to help. Unfortunately, in my company we do not have API Management licensed in our SAP Integration Suite tenant. Even though the UI shows “Integrations and APIs”, when I try to create API artifacts I receive the following error: “API artifacts are not available on your tenant. Availability of these artifacts depends upon the SAP Integration Suite service plan that you use. For more information about different service plans, see SAP Note 2903776.” Because of this, using API Management is not currently an option, and my company will not extend the license. What I have managed to do so far is establish the connectivity between SAP Gateway and BTP through SAP Cloud Connector. I created the system mapping as a Non-SAP system (because using ABAP System did not work for me when connecting to BTP, although I am not sure why). After configuring the virtual host and resource /sap/opu/odata, the tunnel between Cloud Connector and BTP works correctly, and the destination test in BTP returns “Backend is reachable”. Since API Management is not available, I read in several places that it should also be possible to expose the OData service through a Cloud Integration iFlow acting as a proxy. However, I have not been able to successfully deploy the iFlow. The flow I attempted is: HTTPS Sender → Start → Request Reply → HTTP Receiver But the HTTP connection between Request Reply and Receiver shows a red error icon, which prevents the iFlow from deploying (I will attach a screenshot). Could you please advise: Whether it is actually possible to expose SAP Gateway OData through Cloud Integration alone (without API Management) in this scenario? If so, what should be the correct connections between Sender → Start, Start → Request Reply, and Request Reply → Receiver? Is there any specific configuration required for the HTTP receiver adapter when using Cloud Connector that might cause the validation error I am seeing? Thank you again for your help — it is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: PM-Revisions not seen in OIOB
entry.content: Hi Anirudh, the reason is a selection issue. Where in ECC the revisions were selected separately table by table, in S4, they are selected in one inner join where one of the talbes is empty. SAP Note 3418241 is helpful here. Please check this SAP Note
